title: Human State Interface (HSI) 1.2
$comment: HSI 1.2 canonical schema. HSI is a contract-only interface. Some reference-integrity constraints MUST be enforced out-of-band for HSI-VALIDATE-STRICT compliance.

observed_at_utc.$comment: Event-time: the human state time (typically aligns with the latest window end).

computed_at_utc.$comment: Processing-time: when this payload was produced. Must be >= observed_at_utc (enforced out-of-band unless validator supports format comparisons).

windows.$comment: Map keys are the authoritative window IDs. axis_reading.window_ids and embedding.window_ids SHOULD reference these keys.

meta.$comment: meta may include provenance and other producer-defined keys. meta.provenance is the canonical place for source tracking in this version.

source_tier.$comment: Signal-fidelity tier declared on a source in meta.provenance.sources. 1 = ground-truth (e.g. beat-to-beat intervals, raw sensor stream), 2 = vendor-derived high-fidelity features, 3 = proxy time-series (trend-level only), 4 = snapshot/minimal signal. Higher tier numbers SHOULD correspond to reduced downstream trust. Readings and embeddings do not carry their own source_tier; consumers derive a reading- or embedding-level tier by resolving evidence_source_ids against meta.provenance.sources and taking the most conservative (highest-numbered) tier among cited sources. See RFC-HSI-0008 §6.7.

window.$comment: HSI-VALIDATE-STRICT: window.end_utc MUST be >= window.start_utc (out-of-band unless validator supports format comparisons).

inference_mode.$comment: probabilistic_model: learned/ensemble. deterministic_rule: formulas/rulepacks. external_provider: third-party or OS facts. composite: merged multi-source.

score.$comment: score MAY be null when a producer cannot compute a reading; consumers MUST NOT treat null as zero.

model_id.$comment: URI-shaped identifier (RFC 3986 scheme + path). Reserved schemes in this contract: 'model://' for learned/ensemble artifacts, 'rulepack://' for deterministic rule bundles, 'provider://' for third-party/platform attributions.

items.$comment: entries SHOULD reference keys in meta.provenance.sources. Resolve signal fidelity via each source's source_tier in meta.provenance.sources.

type.$comment: Role of the source — what kind of thing produced the signal. Orthogonal to `device_class` (physical form factor), `signals` (what is measured), and `transport` (how the bytes reach the producer).

source_tier.$comment: Signal-fidelity tier for this source. Axis readings and embeddings resolve fidelity via evidence_source_ids → meta.provenance.sources.*.source_tier.

device_class.$comment: Physical form factor. Only meaningful when `type` is a hardware-bearing role (typically `sensor`). Use `other` for unusual form factors; open an RFC before adding new canonical values so producers and consumers stay aligned.

signals.$comment: Physical or logical signals this source produces. Free-form lowercase tokens; suggested canonical names: `hrv`, `rr`, `ecg`, `ppg`, `spo2`, `resp`, `accel`, `gyro`, `mag`, `temp`, `eda`, `gsr`, `eeg`, `emg`, `touch`, `audio`. Consumers SHOULD tolerate unknown entries (forward-compatibility).

transport.$comment: How data reaches the producer. `inproc` = same-process APIs (e.g. platform sensor SDKs). `network` = generic IP when no more specific transport applies. Use `other` for unusual transports; open an RFC before adding canonical values.

vendor.$comment: Free-form vendor/manufacturer tag (e.g. `polar`, `whoop`, `garmin`, `apple`). Not an enum — keeps the schema stable as new vendors ship. Non-normative; consumers SHOULD NOT branch critical logic on this value. PRIVACY: vendor is the highest-entropy field in `source` and is the most correlatable with demographics (brand choice tracks income, tech-adoption patterns, region). It is not PII per GDPR/HIPAA, but it weakens the anti-fingerprinting posture implied by `privacy.contains_pii: false` when combined with session identifiers or cross-payload correlation. Producers SHOULD omit `vendor` in privacy-sensitive contexts — especially when `privacy.purposes` is research-only and payloads may be joined across subjects. Keep it when the consumer genuinely needs vendor-specific signal calibration (e.g., HRV algorithm differences between Polar and Garmin).

sources.$comment: Map keys are the authoritative source IDs. evidence_source_ids on axis_reading and embeddings SHOULD reference these keys.

equation_id.$comment: Optional identifier for the computation formula used to derive readings in this payload.

merge_rule_id.$comment: Optional identifier for the merge rule applied when combining multi-source readings.

srm_snapshot_id.$comment: Optional identifier for a state-representation-model snapshot used to interpret this payload.

vector.$comment: Include only when privacy allows. Producers that want a portable integrity identifier SHOULD also include vector_hash computed over a canonical encoding of vector (see vector_hash).

vector_hash.$comment: Stable integrity identifier, algorithm-prefixed (currently sha256 lowercase hex). Computed over a canonical encoding of the embedding content; RFC 8785 (JCS) is RECOMMENDED when hashing the vector array. Recommended even if vector is present.

items.$comment: entries SHOULD reference keys in meta.provenance.sources.

evidence_source_ids.$comment: Mirrors axis_reading.evidence_source_ids so producers can declare which sources backed this embedding. Consumers resolve each id against meta.provenance.sources for type/quality/source_tier.

purposes.$comment: Open-ended array of strings. Producers are free to use any string they want, as long as it is not PII. examples: 'research', 'personalization', 'safety_monitoring', 'clinical_support', 'product_telemetry', 'other'
